place: Finesville CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Finesville’s name comes from the Lenape term meaning "stream between split hills," echoing the area’s early Native American heritage.
Pohatcong Township, where Finesville sits, was officially created in 1881 and incorporated the next year, carving itself from Greenwich Township.
The Norfolk Southern Railway’s Lehigh Line, once the mainline of the Lehigh Valley Railroad, still cuts through Finesville on its way to Phillipsburg.
In 1911 the township’s industrial core split off to become Alpha borough, leaving Finesville surrounded by a doughnut town.
The Phillipsburg Mall straddles the border of Pohatcong and Lopatcong, with 43 acres of its property lying within Finesville’s limits.
Finesville is governed under the Faulkner Act’s small‑municipality form, a structure that shapes local decision‑making.
